what_it_does: Records metadata for a channel attachment, specifically an image asset linked to the design-assets channel.
observations: The file consists only of YAML frontmatter with no body content
standouts: Target URI contains spaces and parentheses: "image (62)"
Uses an image:// scheme to reference the asset
concerns: Target URI contains unescaped spaces and parentheses, which may cause parsing or linking issues
File contains no alt text, dimensions, or file size
Only metadata is present, so the attachment record is not self-describing beyond the target URI
notes: A bare attachment ledger entry that maps a design-assets channel image attachment to an image:// URI and a 2026 timestamp.
questions: What does the "image (62)" filename refer to?
Why is the added timestamp in 2026?
Are there other attachment types besides images in this channel?
Is the target URI expected to be resolvable by a specific asset store?
purpose_vs_actual: The file appears intended to record an image attachment for a channel, and its frontmatter matches that purpose.
intention_signals: Catalog an image asset attached to the design-assets channel so it can be referenced by a stable attachment id rather than by its filesystem-like path.
contribution: Provides a concrete, timestamped attachment record linking a channel to an image asset via a URI target, which a fused system could retain as a canonical reference entry.
summary_for_embedding: A YAML-frontmatter attachment ledger file that records an image asset in the design-assets channel, identified as att-efbe137e, with an image://design-assets/icons/image (62) target and a 2026-06-22 timestamp.
intention_for_embedding: To register a channel image attachment so it can be looked up by id and associated with its source asset URI.
